key_insight: Plasma proteins appear to mediate how cardiometabolic diseases such as diabetes, hypertension, and dyslipidemia lead to downstream cardiovascular, neurological, renal, and pulmonary complications.
clinical_relevance: Identified proteins could improve risk stratification and point to biomarker-driven targets for earlier detection or intervention in people with primary cardiometabolic disease.
method: Mediation analysis across 53,030 UK Biobank participants linked primary cardiometabolic diseases to 18 secondary outcomes.
Mendelian randomization tested whether key proteins may play causal roles.
Machine learning evaluated whether adding mediator proteins improved prediction beyond traditional clinical factors.
evidence_signal: The study found 998 significant mediation pathways involving 337 unique proteins, with predictive models improving when mediator proteins were included.
watch_item: These are association-based findings from cohort data, so clinical use will require external validation and testing in prospective studies before implementation.
paper_title: Plasma Proteomics Linking Primary and Secondary diseases: Insights into Molecular Mediation from UK Biobank Data
